name: to_vec_always_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_to_vec_always_empty(h: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        h.spec_to_vec()@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: to_vec should not always return an empty vector for every marshalable hash map

name: to_vec_at_most_one
code:
proof fn phi_2_to_vec_at_most_one(h: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        h.spec_to_vec()@.len() <= 1,
{
}
reason: Hash maps can contain many entries so to_vec should be able to return more than one element

name: to_vec_constant_function
code:
proof fn phi_3_to_vec_constant_function(h1: CKeyHashMap, h2: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h1.is_marshalable(),
        h2.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        h1.spec_to_vec()@ =~= h2.spec_to_vec()@,
{
}
reason: Different hash maps should generally produce different to_vec results so a constant function implies total information loss

name: to_vec_all_elements_identical
code:
proof fn phi_4_to_vec_all_elements_identical(h: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h.is_marshalable(),
        h.spec_to_vec()@.len() >= 2,
    ensures
        h.spec_to_vec()@[0] === h.spec_to_vec()@[1],
{
}
reason: A hash map with multiple distinct entries should produce distinct key-value pairs in to_vec not identical copies

name: to_vec_ignores_content
code:
proof fn phi_5_to_vec_ignores_content(h1: CKeyHashMap, h2: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h1.is_marshalable(),
        h2.is_marshalable(),
        h1.ghost_serialize() !== h2.ghost_serialize(),
    ensures
        h1.spec_to_vec()@ =~= h2.spec_to_vec()@,
{
}
reason: Maps with different serialized content represent different data so to_vec must reflect that difference in its output
